name: Amygdala
location: Deep within anterior temporal lobe, rostral to hippocampus
description: Almond-shaped nuclear complex that serves as the brain's emotional threat sentinel and fear-conditioning hub.
functions: Fear conditioning and threat detection (Basolateral complex)
Autonomic fight-or-flight initiation via Central Nucleus output to Hypothalamus
Emotional memory valence tagging and facial expression evaluation
bloodSupply: Anterior Choroidal Artery (branch of Internal Carotid)
clinicalImportance: Hyperactive in PTSD and panic disorders; bilateral calcification leads to complete absence of fear recognition (Patient S.M.).
researchNotes: Low-road subcortical pathway (Thalamus -> Amygdala) processes threat cues in under 20 milliseconds.
interestingFacts: In response to a threat, the amygdala fires faster than conscious visual awareness.
